description: Back Down the Ridge to Station F

tokens: And to rephrase the question earlier, on the way back down, you might integrate any distinction in the lithology on the way back with a better Sun angle and you're free to take grab samples en route to Weird.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Al, I think that's Weird to the north...I mean just to the left of (the line of sight to) North Triplet.
And North Triplet appears to me to be right behind the LM (meaning 'aft of the LM').
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It's halfway between those two large boulders and one way down.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) Okay, these rocks, or these boulders, in this field here appear to be very weathered, obviously not by (chuckles) atmosphere, but eroded by some process, because they all show cracks.
They show evidences of being broken up either by impact or subsequently.
And it looks to me as though these rocks are really pretty old.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And do you have anything left on the 16-millimeter or has it been running on the MET.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We might turn it on now and follow the progress.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Have you checked the setting on it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Without taking any extra time, if you come across any boulders large enough, we might fill the comm check (square) on the way down.
If you haven't already done that on the way up.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I don't think we're going to find any along our path big enough, Fredo.
The very largest ones are off to the right - south of us a bit (means north) - (garbled) up the hill a bit more.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Are you on the thing back there?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Want me to hold you back?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me grab it, you're going to go over here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Right here again, Houston, the texture here appears to be...The regolith appears to be a lot of pebbles, approximately a quarter of an inch on down, that go along with the fines.
And the same textured pattern we spoke of before and photographed is also here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Why don't we stop here to see if we're really going to Weird.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) Man, the LM doesn't seem like it's getting much closer.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Is that Weird right down there, do you think?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: No, Weird is...Let's see; Weird is almost due east of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: 3.75 and 45 percent; and I'm on Min(imum)...
(correcting himself) Medium flow, and I'm comfortable.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm on 3.75; Min flow, 40 percent, and very comfortable.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: With the boulder in the near foreground.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Long Pause as they start moving again)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're now out of the boulder field, Houston, and proceeding on down the flank.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, I believe I'll just get a shot...Let's get a sample of that baby right there.
Let's grab some from that one.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're just going to get a quick grab here of a rock, and I'll photograph it because it's got some tremendous fillets on it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Don't hit the fillets until I photograph it.
(Garbled) and let me get a quick shot there.
Get a quick pan across there.
(Pause) That looks like...Yeah, we ought to get a piece of that baby.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Oh, man; that's hard, hard, hard!
Look at that (impact) melt in it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) Okay, here's a piece of it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, that was about...(Pause) It's about where we...No, I guess not.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: This darn bag dispenser is not doing what it's supposed to do.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, the rock we're taking is in 14-N.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Large filleted rock that Al photographed.
Do you want me to pull awhile?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We don't want anything to drop off.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And you want me to hold back a while?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: No, no, let's just let it run.
(Pause) Long as we don't lose anything.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, it's holding in very well.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (center of gravity) now than we had before with that big rock in there.
(Long Pause) Fredo, can you give us an idea at about what time we should arrive at Weird?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That 16-millimeter (camera) is bouncing all over every place.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It's taken photos from every view.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I hate to make a grab here that's not from this crater.
It looks like that cut fairly deep, though.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let's...Hey, here's a whole batch of them right down here, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Off to the left and ahead.
They're all from this same area.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Unable to see any stratigraphy in any of these craters.
The slumping has been such that it's pretty much destroyed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll grab this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, positionwise, you're past Flank now.
Or at least abeam position of Flank?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: No, we're not at Flank yet.
I'd say we're probably 15 minutes away from Weird.
Did you get it on board?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: As a matter of fact, I think this is Flank right here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, I've got the rock on board.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And one other question that's up here is to check for the stratigraphy reported earlier of the light gray-white layer below the top, if you see that exposed anywhere.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Now, we did not see that until we started approaching the edge of the boulder field.
There's no evidence of that at all that we noticed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: One thing I did notice further outside of where we saw the white underneath; it looked like an impact had either been of the white rock or it was a splatter of white.
And it was just outside where Al was reporting that the underlying layer was white.
As a matter of fact...No, that's just...The Sun angle was causing it.
Right now, some of the spray that we're kicking up looks white underneath, but I'm convinced it's just the (Sun) angle.
I looked back the other way, and it's not substantiated.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That's what I'm trying to do!
(Long Pause) Okay, we're moving along pretty well, Fred, at this point.
And I'd say we're still probably about 10 minutes away from Weird.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Looks kind of like you're making a little better time going down than up.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Yeah, the slope's a different way, Fredo.
(Pause) In this case, the MET helps.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, don't let me lose that baby (meaning Weird Crater).
That's it right there with the three...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're getting down to the place where we won't be able to see it.
(Pause) This is probably Flank right here, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm not going to say until I get down and look at the exact pattern.
But if this is really Flank, we should have been at the top of Cone Crater where we were.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think we've already passed Flank.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It maybe looks down here, Ed, that may be what you're looking at there, if you've got another Flank-size crater, is the one by (Station) E.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: No, this is a big crater.
It has a fairly sharp crater in the south edge of it, which is...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, that looks like it may be the one by E.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Listens) Yeah, I think that's it, Fredo.
And it's...Oh, it's at least 50 or 60 feet deep.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Why don't we just grab a couple from right here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, it's falling apart as you pick it up; very crumbly, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Very, very soft rock from the rim of that crater, plus another one fairly close to it with crystal in it, that's flashing now; going into bag...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Not quite; let me get it in there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Stay behind me; we don't want to lose anything now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay; that's where we're going, right there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, we're going right for Weird.
Head right for the big boulder.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: One of the problems of going down hill here is that you have essentially diffraction, I guess, around your body, and it creates a halo effect in your shadow, and you just can't see a darn thing right in front of you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It's either blacked out or washed out right immediately down-Sun of you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Fred, we're still moving, and MET's about 3 minutes away now from Weird.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: The crater we are going by now - we're just to the north of it, Fredo - is an old subdued crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you want to run over behind that boulder over there, and I'll try and talk to you.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You're the one that has to get behind it and try to talk to Houston.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) On second thought, maybe it's not big enough.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Sure is a big old boulder!
I'll take a picture of it anyway.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, and this big boulder, Al, is...You're just about at Weird now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Oh, probably a couple of hundred meters short of Weird.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: This country is so rolling and undulating, Fred, with rises and dips everywhere, that you can be going by a fairly good-size crater and not even recognize it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (To Ed) Okay, I'm back with you.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I think this is Weird to our right here.
See that fresh one right there?
I think that's the fresh one of the Weird pattern.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Al and Ed; on the Weird task, we'd like a pan and grab samples at Weird.
And we'll pick up most of our tasks that we had bypassed at E when we get to Triplet.
transcript.speaker: Haise
